SEARCH
Package minum.web

Class Server

java.lang.Object
minum.web.Server
All Implemented Interfaces:
AutoCloseable
public class Server extends Object implements AutoCloseable
The purpose here is to make it marginally easier to work with a ServerSocket.

First, instantiate this class using a running serverSocket Then, by running the start method, we gain access to the server's socket. This way we can easily test / control the server side but also tie it in with an ExecutorService for controlling lots of server threads.

  • Field Details

    • centralLoopFuture

      public Future<?> centralLoopFuture
      This is the future returned when we submitted the thread for the central server loop to the ExecutorService

  • Constructor Details

    • Server

      public Server(ServerSocket ss, Context context, String serverName, TheBrig theBrig)

  • Method Details

    • start

      public void start(ExecutorService es, ThrowingConsumer<ISocketWrapper,IOException> handler)
      This is the infinite loop running inside the basic socket server code. Every time we ServerSocket.accept() a new incoming socket connection, we attach our "end of the phone line" to the code that is "handler", which mainly handles it from there.
      Parameters:
      handler - the commonest handler will be found at WebFramework.makePrimaryHttpHandler(java.util.function.Function<minum.web.StartLine, java.util.function.Function<minum.web.Request, minum.web.Response>>)

    • close

      public void close() throws IOException
      Specified by:
      close in interface AutoCloseable
      Throws:
      IOException

    • getHost

      public String getHost()

    • getPort

      public int getPort()

    • getServer

      public ISocketWrapper getServer(ISocketWrapper sw)
      This is a helper method to find the server SocketWrapper connected to a client SocketWrapper.

    • removeMyRecord

      public void removeMyRecord(ISocketWrapper socketWrapper)
      When we first create a SocketWrapper in Server, we provide it a reference back to this object, so that it can call this command. This class maintains a list of open sockets in setOfSWs, and allows generated SocketWrappers to deregister themselves from this list by using this method.

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
